title: 2.7.7 Anti Surge Support [On] This item allows you to enable or disable the Anti
text:
# 2.7.7 Anti Surge Support [On] This item allows you to enable or disable the Anti Surge function.
Configuration options: [On] [Off]

title: 2.8 Boot menu The Boot menu items allow you to change the system boot options. S
text:
# 2.8 Boot menu The Boot menu items allow you to change the system boot options. Scroll down to display the other BIOS items.
[[PIC_MISSING]]

title: 2.8.1 Fast Boot [Enabled] [Enabled] Select to accelerate the boot speed. [Disabl
text: # 2.8.1 Fast Boot [Enabled] [Enabled] Select to accelerate the boot speed. [Disabled] Select to go back to normal boot speed. The following five items appear when you set Fast Boot to [Enabled].

title: 2.8.2 Next Boot after AC Power Loss [Normal Boot] [Normal Boot] Returns to norma
text: # 2.8.2 Next Boot after AC Power Loss [Normal Boot] [Normal Boot] Returns to normal boot on the next boot after AC power loss. [Fast Boot] Accelerates the boot speed on the next boot after AC power loss.

title: 2.8.3 Boot Logo Display [Auto] [Auto] Adjusts logo automatically based on displa
text: # 2.8.3 Boot Logo Display [Auto] [Auto] Adjusts logo automatically based on display re qur em ents. [Full Screen] Maximize the boot logo size. [Disabled] Hide the logo during POST.

title: POST Delay Time [3 sec] This item appears only when you set Boot Logo Display to
text: # POST Delay Time [3 sec] This item appears only when you set Boot Logo Display to [Auto] and [Full Screen]. This item allows you to select the desired additional POST waiting time to easily enter the BIOS setup. You can only execute the POST delay time during Normal Boot. The values range from 0 to 10 seconds. This feature will only work under normal boot.

title: Post Report [5 sec] This item appears only when you set Boot Logo Display to [Di
text: # Post Report [5 sec] This item appears only when you set Boot Logo Display to [Disabled]. This item allows you to select a desired post report waiting time. Configuration options: [1 sec] $\\sim$ [10 sec] [Until Press ESC].

title: 2.8.4 Boot up NumLock State [Enabled] This item allows you to enable or disable
text: # 2.8.4 Boot up NumLock State [Enabled] This item allows you to enable or disable power-on state of the NumLock. Configuration options: [Disabled] [Enabled]

title: 2.8.5 Above 4GB MMIO BIOS assignment [Disabled] This item allows you to enable o
text: # 2.8.5 Above 4GB MMIO BIOS assignment [Disabled] This item allows you to enable or disable boot with initialization of a minimal set of devices required to launch active boot option. This item is ineffective for BBS boot options. Configuration options: [Disabled] [Enabled]

title: 2.8.6 Wait for \u2018F1\u2019 If Error [Enabled] When this item is set to [Enabl
text: # 2.8.6 Wait for \u2018F1\u2019 If Error [Enabled] When this item is set to [Enabled], the system waits for the F1 key to be pressed when error occurs. Configuration options: [Disabled] [Enabled]

title: 2.8.7 Option ROM Messages [Force BIOS] [Force BIOS] The third-party ROM messages
text: # 2.8.7 Option ROM Messages [Force BIOS] [Force BIOS] The third-party ROM messages will be displayed during POST. [Keep Current] Disables the ROM messages during POST.

title: 2.8.8 Interrupt 19 Capture [Disabled] This item allows you to trap Interrupt 19
text: # 2.8.8 Interrupt 19 Capture [Disabled] This item allows you to trap Interrupt 19 by the option ROMs. Configuration options: [Disabled] [Enabled]

title: 2.8.9 Setup Mode [EZ Mode] [Advanced Mode] This item allows you to go to Advance
text: # 2.8.9 Setup Mode [EZ Mode] [Advanced Mode] This item allows you to go to Advanced Mode of the BIOS after POST. [EZ Mode] This item allows you to go to EZ Mode of the BIOS after POST.

title: 2.8.10 CSM (Compatibility Support Module) Allows you to configure the CSM (Compa
text: # 2.8.10 CSM (Compatibility Support Module) Allows you to configure the CSM (Compatibility Support Module) items to fully support the various VGA, bootable devices and add-on devices for better compatibility.

title: Launch CSM [Enabled] [Auto] The system automatically detects the bootable device
text: # Launch CSM [Enabled] [Auto] The system automatically detects the bootable devices and the add-on devices. [Enabled] For a better compatibility, enable the CSM to fully support the non-UEFI driver add-on devices or the UEFI mode. [Disabled] Disable the CSM to fully support the Security Update and Security Boot. The following four items appear when you set Launch CSM to [Enabled].

title: Boot Device Control [UEFI and Legacy OPROM] Allows you to select the type of dev
text: # Boot Device Control [UEFI and Legacy OPROM] Allows you to select the type of devices that you want to boot up. Configuration options: [UEFI and Legacy OPROM] [Legacy OPROM only] [UEFI only]

title: Boot from Network Devices [Legacy only] Allows you to select the type of network
text: # Boot from Network Devices [Legacy only] Allows you to select the type of network devices that you want to launch. Configuration options: [Ignore] [Legacy only] [UEFI driver first]

title: Boot from Storage Devices [Legacy Only] Allows you to select the type of storage
text: # Boot from Storage Devices [Legacy Only] Allows you to select the type of storage devices that you want to launch. Configuration options: [Ignore] [Legacy only] [UEFI driver first]

title: Boot from PCI-E Expansion Devices [Legacy Only] Allows you to select the type of
text: # Boot from PCI-E Expansion Devices [Legacy Only] Allows you to select the type of PCIe/PCI expansion devices that you want to launch. Configuration options: [Legacy only] [UEFI driver first]

title: 2.8.11 Secure Boot Allows you to configure the Secure Boot settings and manage i
text: # 2.8.11 Secure Boot Allows you to configure the Secure Boot settings and manage its keys to protect the system from unauthorized access and malwares during POST.

title: OS Type [ UEFI mode] Allows you to select your installed operating system. [ UEF
text: # OS Type [ UEFI mode] Allows you to select your installed operating system. [ UEFI mode] This item allows you to select your installed operating system. Execute the Secure Boot check. Only select this option when booting on UEFI mode or other Secure Boot compliant OS. [Other OS] Get the optimized function when booting on non-UEFI mode. Secure Boot only supports UEFI mode.

title: Key Management This allows you to manage the Secure Boot keys.
text: # Key Management This allows you to manage the Secure Boot keys.

title: Install Default Secure Boot keys This item allows you to immediately load the de
text: # Install Default Secure Boot keys This item allows you to immediately load the default Security Boot keys, Platform key (PK), Key-exchange Key (KEK), Signature database (db), and Revoked Signatures (dbx). When the default Secure boot keys are loaded, all the Secure Boot keys\u2019 state will change from Unloaded mode to loaded mode.

title: Save Secure Boot Keys This item allows you to save all the Secure Boot keys to a
text: # Save Secure Boot Keys This item allows you to save all the Secure Boot keys to a USB storage device.

title: PK Management The Platform Key (PK) locks and secures the firmware from any non-
text: # PK Management The Platform Key (PK) locks and secures the firmware from any non-permissible changes. The system verifies the PK before your system enters the OS.

title: Set New Key This item allows you to load the downloaded PK from a USB storage de
text: # Set New Key This item allows you to load the downloaded PK from a USB storage device.

title: Delete key Allows you to delete the PK from your system. Once the PK is deleted,
text:
# Delete key Allows you to delete the PK from your system. Once the PK is deleted, all the system\u2019s Secure Boot keys will not be active. Configuration options: [Yes] [No]
The PK file must be formatted as a public key certificate or UEFI variable structure with time-based authenticated variable.

title: KEK Management The KEK (Key-exchange Key or Key Enrollment Key) manages the Sign
text: # KEK Management The KEK (Key-exchange Key or Key Enrollment Key) manages the Signature database (db) and Revoked Signature database (dbx). Key-exchange Key (KEK) refers to Secure Boot Key-Enrollment Key (KEK).

title: Set New Key Allows you to load the downloaded KEK from a USB storage device.
text: # Set New Key Allows you to load the downloaded KEK from a USB storage device.

title: Append Key Allows you to load the additional KEK from a storage device for an ad
text: # Append Key Allows you to load the additional KEK from a storage device for an additional db and dbx loaded management. The KEK file must be formatted as a public key certificate or UEFI variable structure with time-based authenticated variable.

title: Delete key Allows you to delete the Key from your system. Configuration options:
text: # Delete key Allows you to delete the Key from your system. Configuration options: [Yes] [No]

title: DB Management The db (Authorized Signature database) lists the signers or images
text: # DB Management The db (Authorized Signature database) lists the signers or images of UEFI applications, operating system loaders, and UEFI drivers that you can load on the single computer.

title: Set New Key Allows you to load the downloaded db from a USB storage device.
text: # Set New Key Allows you to load the downloaded db from a USB storage device.

title: Append Key Allows you to load the additional KEK from a storage device for an ad
text: # Append Key Allows you to load the additional KEK from a storage device for an additional db and dbx loaded management. The KEK file must be formatted as a public key certificate or UEFI variable structure with time-based authenticated variable.

title: Delete key Allows you to delete the Key from your system. Configuration options:
text: # Delete key Allows you to delete the Key from your system. Configuration options: [Yes] [No]

title: DBX Management The DBX (Revoked Signature database) lists the forbidden images o
text: # DBX Management The DBX (Revoked Signature database) lists the forbidden images of db items that are no longer trusted and cannot be loaded.

title: Set New Key Allows you to load the downloaded dbx from a USB storage device.
text: # Set New Key Allows you to load the downloaded dbx from a USB storage device.

title: Append Key Allows you to load the additional KEK from a storage device for an ad
text: # Append Key Allows you to load the additional KEK from a storage device for an additional db and dbx loaded management. The KEK file must be formatted as a public key certificate or UEFI variable structure with time-based authenticated variable.

title: Delete key Allows you to delete the Key from your system. Configuration options:
text: # Delete key Allows you to delete the Key from your system. Configuration options: [Yes] [No]

title: 2.8.12 Boot Option Priorities These items specify the boot device priority seque
text: # 2.8.12 Boot Option Priorities These items specify the boot device priority sequence from the available devices. The number of device items that appears on the screen depends on the number of devices installed in the system. \u2022 To access OS in Safe Mode, press $\\scriptstyle<\\mathsf{F}8>$ after POST ( 8 not supported). \u2022 To select the boot device during system startup, press $\\scriptstyle{<\\!\\mathsf{F}8\\!>}$ when Logo appears.

title: 2.8.13 Boot Override These items displays the available devices. The number of d
text: # 2.8.13 Boot Override These items displays the available devices. The number of device items that appears on the screen depends on the number of devices installed in the system. Click an item to start booting from the selected device.

title: 2.9 Tool menu The Tool menu items allow you to configure options for special fun
text:
# 2.9 Tool menu The Tool menu items allow you to configure options for special functions. Select an item then press <Enter> to display the submenu.
[[PIC_MISSING]]

title: 2.9.1 EZ Flash 3 Utility Allows you to run EZ Flash 3. Press [Enter] to launch t
text: # 2.9.1 EZ Flash 3 Utility Allows you to run EZ Flash 3. Press [Enter] to launch the EZ Flash 3 screen. For more details, see section 2.1.2 EZ Flash 3 .

title: 2.9.2 Setup Animator [Disabled] Enables or disables the Setup animator. Configur
text: # 2.9.2 Setup Animator [Disabled] Enables or disables the Setup animator. Configuration options: [Disabled] [Enabled]

title: 2.9.3 Overclocking Profile This item allows you to store or load multiple BIOS s
text: # 2.9.3 Overclocking Profile This item allows you to store or load multiple BIOS settings. The Overclocking Profile items show Not assigned if no profile is created.

title: Load from Profile Allows you to load the previous BIOS settings saved in the BIO
text:
# Load from Profile Allows you to load the previous BIOS settings saved in the BIOS Flash. Key in the profile number that saved your CMOS settings, press <Enter>, and then select Yes. \u2022 DO NOT shut down or reset the system while updating the BIOS to prevent the system boot failure!
\u2022 We recommend that you update the BIOS file only coming from the same memory/ CPU configuration and BIOS version.

title: Profile Name Allows you to create a name for the profile you want to save.
text: # Profile Name Allows you to create a name for the profile you want to save.

title: Save to Profile Allows you to save the current BIOS settings to the BIOS Flash,
text: # Save to Profile Allows you to save the current BIOS settings to the BIOS Flash, and create a profile. Key in a profile number from one to eight, press <Enter>, and then select Yes.

title: Load/Save Profile from/to USB Drive This item allows you to load or save profile
text: # Load/Save Profile from/to USB Drive This item allows you to load or save profile from your USB drive, load and save profile to your USB drive.

title: 2.9.4 SPD Information DIMM Slot Number [DIMM_A1] Displays the Serial Presence De
text: # 2.9.4 SPD Information DIMM Slot Number [DIMM_A1] Displays the Serial Presence Detect (SPD) information of the DIMM module installed on the selected slot. Configuration options: [DIMM_A1] [DIMM_B1] [DIMM_A2] [DIMM_B2]

title: 2.10 Exit menu The Exit menu items allow you to load the optimal default values
text:
# 2.10 Exit menu The Exit menu items allow you to load the optimal default values for the BIOS items, and save or discard your changes to the BIOS items.
[[PIC_MISSING]]

title: Load Optimized Defaults This option allows you to load the default values for ea
text: # Load Optimized Defaults This option allows you to load the default values for each of the parameters on the Setup menus. When you select this option or if you press $\\scriptstyle<\\mathsf{F}5>$ , a confirmation window appears. Select OK to load the default values.

title: Save Changes & Reset Once you are finished making your selections, choose this o
text: # Save Changes & Reset Once you are finished making your selections, choose this option from the Exit menu to ensure the values you selected are saved. When you select this option or if you press $\\scriptstyle<\\mathsf{F}10>$ , a confirmation window appears. Select OK to save changes and exit.

title: Discard Changes and Exit This option allows you to exit the Setup program withou
text: # Discard Changes and Exit This option allows you to exit the Setup program without saving your changes. When you select this option or if you press <Esc>, a confirmation window appears. Select OK to discard changes and exit.

title: Launch EFI Shell from USB drives This option allows you to attempt to launch the
text: # Launch EFI Shell from USB drives This option allows you to attempt to launch the EFI Shell application (shellx64.efi) from one of the available USB devices.

title: 2.11 Installing an operating system Motherboard settings and hardware options va
text: # 2.11 Installing an operating system Motherboard settings and hardware options vary. The setup procedures presented in this chapter are for reference only. Refer to operating system documentation for detailed information.

title: 2.11.1 OS 7 and USB 3.0 driver for 100 Series Based on the chipset specification
text: # 2.11.1 OS 7 and USB 3.0 driver for 100 Series Based on the chipset specification, the 100 series requires USB 3.0 drivers to be preloaded in order to use USB keyboard/mouse during OS 7 installation. This section is a guide on preloading USB 3.0 drivers and installing OS 7.

title: Method 1: Using SATA ODD & USB devices Load USB 3.0 drivers using the support DV
text: # Method 1: Using SATA ODD & USB devices Load USB 3.0 drivers using the support DVD and install OS 7 using a USB device.

title: Requirement: \u2022 1 x support DVD \u2022 1 x OS 7 installation source \u2022 1
text:
# Requirement: \u2022 1 x support DVD \u2022 1 x OS 7 installation source \u2022 1 x SATA ODD \u2022 1 x USB device (ODD or storage) The USB storage device requires 8 GB or more capacity. It is recommended to format the storage device before use. 1. Insert the OS 7 installation DVD into a USB ODD, or copy all files on the OS 7 installation DVD to a USB storage device on a working system. 2. Connect the USB ODD or USB storage device to your 100 series platform. 3. Insert the support DVD into a SATA ODD on your 100 series platform. 4. Power on your system and press F8 during POST (Power-On Self Test) to enter the boot screen.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
6. The USB 3.0 driver will be loaded automatically during installation startup.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
The \u201cSetup is starting...\u201d screen will show up if the USB 3.0 driver is loaded correctly. 7. Follow the onscreen instructions to complete the OS 7 installation.

title: Method 2: Using a modified 7 ISO Load USB 3.0 drivers and install OS 7 using a m
text: # Method 2: Using a modified 7 ISO Load USB 3.0 drivers and install OS 7 using a modified OS 7 installation DVD.

title: Requirement: \u2022 1 x support DVD \u2022 1 x OS 7 installation source \u2022 1
text:
# Requirement: \u2022 1 x support DVD \u2022 1 x OS 7 installation source \u2022 1 x Working system (PC or notebook) \u2022 1 x SATA ODD 1. On your working system, create an ISO image file of the OS 7 installation source using a third-party ISO software. 2. Copy both \u201cAuto Un attend.xml\u201d and \u201cAuto Un attend\u201d folder from the root directory of the supporting DVD to your system. 3. Edit the ISO file and add both \u201cAuto Un attend.xml\u201d and \u201cAuto Un attend\u201d folder into the ISO file. 4. Burn this ISO file onto an empty DVD to create a modified OS 7 installation DVD. 5. Insert the modified OS 7 installation DVD into an ODD on your 100 series platform. 6. Power on your system and press F8 during POST (Power-On Self Test) to enter the boot screen. 7. Select the ODD as the boot device. 8. The USB 3.0 driver will be loaded automatically during installation startup.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
The \u201cSetup is starting...\u201d screen will show up if the USB 3.0 driver is loaded correctly. 9. Follow the onscreen instructions to complete the OS 7 installation.

title: Requirement: \u2022 1 x support DVD \u2022 1 x OS 7 installation source \u2022 1
text:
Method 3: Using EZ Installer Use the EZ Installer to create a modified OS 7 installation source. Requirement: \u2022 1 x support DVD \u2022 1 x OS 7 installation DVD \u2022 1 x Working system (PC or notebook) \u2022 1 x SATA ODD \u2022 1 x USB storage device (8 GB or more) 1. Insert the OS 7 installation DVD. 2. Launch the EZ Installer located on the support DVD. 3. Select a method of creating a modified OS 7 installation file: \u2022 7 OS disk to USB storage device - Select 7 OS disk to USB storage device then click Next .
[[PIC_MISSING]]
- Check I agree and then click Next .
[[PIC_MISSING]]
- Select the source of the OS 7 installation disk then click Next .
[[PIC_MISSING]]
- Select the USB storage device and click next .
[[PIC_MISSING]]
Click the refresh icon if the USB storage device is not displayed. - Click Yes to clear the contents on the USB storage device and create a bootable USB device. Make sure to backup contents on the USB storage device, as it will be formatted. - Once completed, click OK to finish.
\u2022 7 OS disk to ISO file
[[PIC_MISSING]]
- Select 7 OS disk to ISO file then click Next - Check I agree and then click Next .
[[PIC_MISSING]]

title: - Select the source of the 7 installation disk then click Next .
text:
# - Select the source of the 7 installation disk then click Next .
[[PIC_MISSING]]
- Select the folder to save the modified OS 7 installation ISO file and click Next .
[[PIC_MISSING]]
- Once completed, click OK to finish. - Burn this ISO file onto an empty DVD to create a modified OS 7 installation DVD. 4. Insert the modified OS 7 installation DVD into an ODD or connect the USB storage device with modified OS 7 installation files onto your 100 series platform. 5. Power on your system and press F8 during POST (Power-On Self Test) to enter the boot screen. 6. Select the ODD or USB storage device as the boot device. 7. The USB 3.0 driver will be loaded automatically during installation startup. The \u201cSetup is starting...\u201d screen will show up if the USB 3.0 driver is loaded correctly. 8. Follow the onscreen instructions to complete the OS 7 installation.

title: REACH Complying with the REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation, and Res
text: # REACH Complying with the REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation, and Restriction of Chemicals) regulatory framework, we published the chemical substances in our products at REACH website. DO NOT throw the motherboard in municipal waste. This product has been designed to enable proper reuse of parts and recycling. This symbol of the crossed out wheeled bin indicates that the product (electrical and electronic equipment) should not be placed in municipal waste. Check local regulations for disposal of electronic products. DO NOT throw the mercury-containing button cell battery in municipal waste. This symbol of the crossed out wheeled bin indicates that the battery should not be placed in municipal waste.

title: 1 Important safety instructions \u2022\u2022 Handsfree activation could suddenly
text: # 1 Important safety instructions \u2022\u2022 Handsfree activation could suddenly increase the volume in the earpiece to a very high level: make sure the handset is not too close to your ear. \u2022\u2022 This equipment is not designed to make emergency calls when the power fails. An alternative has to be made available to allow emergency calls. \u2022\u2022 Do not allow the product to come into contact with liquids. \u2022\u2022 Do not use any cleaning agents containing alcohol, ammonia, benzene, or abrasives as these may harm the set. \u2022\u2022 Do not expose the phone to excessive heat caused by heating equipment or direct sunlight. \u2022\u2022 Do not drop your phone or allow objects to fall on your phone. \u2022\u2022 Active mobile phones in the vicinity may cause interference.

title: Power requirements
text:
# Power requirements
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022 This product requires an electrical supply of 100-240 volts AC. In case of power failure, the communication can be lost. \u2022 The voltage on the network is classified as TNV-3 (Telecommunication Network Voltages), as defined in the standard EN 60950.

title: About operating and storage temperatures
text: # About operating and storage temperatures

title: Warning \u2022 Operate in a place where temperature is always between $0^{\\circ
text: # Warning \u2022 Operate in a place where temperature is always between $0^{\\circ}\\mathrm{C}$ to $+40^{\\circ}\\mathrm{C}$ (up to $90\\%$ relative humidity). \u2022 Store in a place where temperature is always between $-20^{\\circ}\\mathrm{C}$ and $+45^{\\circ}\\bigsubset$ (up to $95\\%$ relative humidity). \u2022 Battery life may be shorter in low temperature conditions. \u2022\u2022 The Electrical network is classified as hazardous. The only way to power down the charger is to unplug the power supply from the electrical outlet. Ensure that the electrical outlet is always easily accessible.

title: To avoid damage or malfunction
text: # To avoid damage or malfunction

title: Caution
text:
# Caution
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 Use only the power supply listed in the user instructions. \u2022\u2022 Use only the batteries listed in the user instructions. \u2022\u2022 Risk of explosion if battery is replaced by an incorrect type. \u2022\u2022 Dispose of used batteries according to the instructions. \u2022\u2022 Do not dispose of batteries in fire. \u2022\u2022 Always use the cables provided with the product. \u2022\u2022 Do not allow the charging contacts or the battery to come into contact with metal objects. \u2022\u2022 Do not let small metal objects come into contact with the product. This can deteriorate audio quality and damage the product. \u2022\u2022 Metallic objects may be retained if placed near or on the handset receiver. \u2022\u2022 Do not use the product in places where there are explosive hazards. \u2022\u2022 Do not open the handset, base station or charger as you could be exposed to high voltages. \u2022\u2022 For pluggable equipment, the socket-outlet shall be installed near the equipment and shall be easily accessible.

# Caution

title: Connect the base station
text: # Connect the base station

title: Warning
text:
# Warning
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 Risk of product damage!
Ensure that the power supply voltage corresponds to the voltage printed on the back or the underside of the phone. \u2022\u2022 Use only the supplied power adapter to charge the batteries.

title: 未命名章节
Note \u2022\u2022 If you subscribe to the digital subscriber line (DSL) high spe
Install the handset The batteries are pre-installed in the handset. Pull the bat
text:
# Install the handset The batteries are pre-installed in the handset. Pull the battery tape off from the battery door before charging.
[[PIC_MISSING]]

title: Caution
text:
# Caution
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 Risk of explosion!
Keep batteries away from heat, sunshine or fire. Never discard batteries in fire. \u2022\u2022 Use only the supplied batteries. \u2022\u2022 Risk of decreased battery life!
Never mix different brands or types of batteries.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 Charge the batteries for 8 hours before first use. \u2022\u2022 If the handset becomes warm when the batteries are charging, it is normal.

title: Warning \u2022\u2022 Check the battery polarity when inserting in the battery co
text: # Warning \u2022\u2022 Check the battery polarity when inserting in the battery compartment. Incorrect polarity may damage the product.

title: Configure your phone (country dependent) 1 When using your phone for the first t
text: # Configure your phone (country dependent) 1 When using your phone for the first time, you see a welcome message. 2 Press [OK] .

title: Set the country/language Select your country/language, then press [OK] to confir
text: # Set the country/language Select your country/language, then press [OK] to confirm.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 The country/language setting option is country dependent. If n
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 The country/language setting option is country dependent. If no welcome message is displayed, it means the country/language setting is preset for your country. Then you can set the date and time. To re-set the language, see the following steps. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Language] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select a language, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Set the date and time 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Date & time] , then
text: # Set the date and time 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Date & time] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Press the numeric buttons to enter the date, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The time setting menu is displayed on the handset. 3 Press the numeric buttons to enter the time. \u2022 If the time is in 12-hour format, press / to select [am] or [pm] (Country dependent). 4 Press [OK] to confirm. Change the remote access PIN code (for XL495)

title: Note
text:
# Note
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 This feature is available only for models with answering machine. \u2022\u2022 The default answering machine remote access PIN code is 0000 and it is important to change it to ensure the security. $\\hookrightarrow$ The country/language setting is saved. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Remote access] $>$ [Change PIN] , then press [OK] to confirm.
2 Enter the old PIN, then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Enter the new PIN code, then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Enter the new PIN code again, then press [Save] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Charge the handset Place the handset on the base station to charge the handset.
text:
# Charge the handset Place the handset on the base station to charge the handset. When the handset is placed correctly on the base station, you hear a docking sound. $\\hookrightarrow$ The handset starts charging.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 Charge the batteries for 8 hours before first use. \u2022\u2022 If the handset becomes warm when the batteries are being charged, it is normal. You can activate or deactivate the docking tone (see 'Set the docking tone' on page 25). Your phone is now ready to use.

title: Check the battery level The battery icon displays the current battery
text:
# Check the battery level The battery icon displays the current battery
[[PIC_MISSING]]
The handset turns off if the batteries are empty. If you are on the phone, you hear warning tones when the batteries are almost empty. The call gets disconnected after the warning.
[[PIC_MISSING]]

title: What is standby mode
text:
# What is standby mode?
Your phone is in standby mode when it is idle. The handset name, date and time, and handset number are displayed on the standby screen.

title: Display icons In standby mode, the icons shown on the main screen tell you what
text:
# Display icons In standby mode, the icons shown on the main screen tell you what features are available on your handset.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
[[PIC_MISSING]]
Check the signal strength The number of bars indicates the connection status between the handset and base station. The more bars are shown, the better the connection is. \u2022 Make sure the handset is connected to the base station before you make or receive calls and carry out the phone functions and features. \u2022 If you hear warning tones when you are on the phone, the handset is almost out of battery or the handset is out of range. Charge the battery or move the handset nearer to the base station.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 When ECO+ is on, the signal strength is not displayed.
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 When ECO+ is on, the signal strength is not displayed.

title: Switch the handset on or off Press and hold to switch the handset on or off.
text: # Switch the handset on or off Press and hold to switch the handset on or off.

title: Tip
text:

\u2022\u2022 Check the signal strength before you make a call or when you are in a call (see 'Check the signal strength' on page 11).

title: Make a call You can make a call in these ways: \u2022 Normal call. \u2022 Predia
text: # Make a call You can make a call in these ways: \u2022 Normal call. \u2022 Predial call. \u2022 Call through the direct keys You can also make a call from the redial list (see 'Redial a call' on page 23), phonebook list (see 'Call from the phonebook' on page 19) and call log (see 'Return a call' on page 21).

title: Normal call 1 Press or . 2 Dial the phone number. $\\hookrightarrow$ The number
text: # Normal call 1 Press or . 2 Dial the phone number. $\\hookrightarrow$ The number is dialed out. $\\hookrightarrow$ The duration of your current call is displayed.

title: Predial call 1 Dial the phone number \u2022 To erase a digit, press [Clear] . \u
text: # Predial call 1 Dial the phone number \u2022 To erase a digit, press [Clear] . \u2022 To enter a pause, press and hold . 2 Press or to dial out the call.

title: Call through the direct key You can make a quick call from the record presaved i
text: # Call through the direct key You can make a quick call from the record presaved in the direct key in the following ways: \u2022 Press m1 , m2 or m3 in standby mode, then press $\\backsim$ or to dial out the number. \u2022 Press $\\backsim$ or to get the dial tone first. Then press m1 , m2 or m3 to display the record content. Then press [OK] to dial out the number.

title: Tip \u2022\u2022 For information of how to set the direct keys, see the chapter
text: # Tip \u2022\u2022 For information of how to set the direct keys, see the chapter 'Phonebook', section 'Direct keys'.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 The talk time of your current call is displayed on the call ti
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 The talk time of your current call is displayed on the call timer. \u2022\u2022 If you hear warning tones, the phone is almost out of battery or out of range. Charge the battery or move the phone close to the base station.

title: Answer a call When the phone rings, you can select from the following options: \
text: # Answer a call When the phone rings, you can select from the following options: \u2022 press $\\backsim$ or to answer the call. \u2022 pick up the phone from the base station (or from the charger) to answer the call when you have activated the auto answer feature (see 'Auto answer' on page 26).

title: Warning \u2022\u2022 When the handset rings or when the handsfree is activated,
text: # Warning \u2022\u2022 When the handset rings or when the handsfree is activated, keep the handset away from your ear to avoid ear damage.

title: Note
text:
# Note
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 The caller ID service is available if you have registered to the caller ID service with your service provider.

title: Make a second call \u2022\u2022 When there is a missed call, a notification mess
text: # Make a second call \u2022\u2022 When there is a missed call, a notification message is displayed. \u2022\u2022 Select [Silent] to turn off the ringer of the current incoming call.

title: End a call You can end a call in these ways: \u2022 Press
text:
# End a call You can end a call in these ways: \u2022 Press ;

title: Adjust the earpiece/speaker volume Press / to adjust the volume during a call. $
text: # Adjust the earpiece/speaker volume Press / to adjust the volume during a call. $\\hookrightarrow$ The earpiece/speaker volume is adjusted and the phone is back to the call screen.

title: Mute the microphone 1 Press during a call. $\\hookrightarrow$ [Mute on] is displ
text: # Mute the microphone 1 Press during a call. $\\hookrightarrow$ [Mute on] is displayed on the handset. $\\hookrightarrow$ The caller cannot hear you, but you can still hear his voice. 2 Press again to unmute the microphone. $\\hookrightarrow$ You can now communicate with the caller.

title: Turn the speaker on or off Note \u2022\u2022 This service is network dependent.
text: # Turn the speaker on or off Note \u2022\u2022 This service is network dependent. 1 Press during a call. $\\hookrightarrow$ The first call is put on hold. 2 Dial the second number. $\\hookrightarrow$ The number displayed on the screen is dialed out.

title: Answer a second call Note \u2022\u2022 This service is network dependent. When t
text: # Answer a second call Note \u2022\u2022 This service is network dependent. When there is a periodical beep to notify you of an incoming call, you can answer the call in these ways: Press and $\\supseteqq$ to answer the call. $\\hookrightarrow$ The first call is put on hold, and you are now connected to the second call. Press and to end the current call and answer the first call.

title: Switch between two calls Note \u2022\u2022 This service is network dependent. Yo
text:
# Switch between two calls Note \u2022\u2022 This service is network dependent. You can switch your calls in these ways: \u2022 Press $\\backsim$ and $\\supseteqq$ ;
or \u2022 Press [Option] and select [Switch calls] , then press [OK] again to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The current call is put on hold, and you are now connected to the other call. Press .

title: Make a conference call with the external callers
text: # Make a conference call with the external callers

title: Note \u2022\u2022 This service is network dependent. Check with the service prov
text:
# Note \u2022\u2022 This service is network dependent. Check with the service provider for additional charges. When you are connected to two calls, you can make a conference call in these ways: \u2022 Press , then $\\exists$ ;
or \u2022 Press [Option] , select [Conference] and then press [OK] again to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The two calls are combined and a conference call is established.

title: 5 Intercom and conference calls
text: # 5 Intercom and conference calls

title: Note
text:
# Note
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 This feature is available for multi- handset versions only. An intercom call is a call to another handset that shares the same base station. A conference call involves a conversation between you, another handset user and the outside callers.

title: Make a call to another handset
text: # Make a call to another handset

title: Note
text:
# Note
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 If the base station only has 2 registered handsets, press and hold to make a call to another handset. 1 Press and hold . $\\hookrightarrow$ For multi-handset versions, the handsets available for intercom are displayed, then go to step 2. $\\hookrightarrow$ For two-handset versions, the other handset rings, then go to step 3. 2 Select a handset, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The selected handset rings. 3 Press on the selected handset. $\\hookrightarrow$ The intercom is established. 4 Press to end the intercom call.

title: While you are on the phone You can go from one handset to another during a call:
text: # While you are on the phone You can go from one handset to another during a call: 1 Press and hold . $\\hookrightarrow$ The current caller is put on hold. $\\hookrightarrow$ For multi-handset versions, the handsets available for intercom are displayed, then go to step 2. 2 Select a handset, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ Wait for the other side to answer your call.

title: Switch between calls Press and hold to switch between the outside call and the i
text: # Switch between calls Press and hold to switch between the outside call and the intercom call.

title: Make a conference call A 3-way conference call is between you, another handset u
text: # Make a conference call A 3-way conference call is between you, another handset user and the outside callers. It requires two handsets to share the same base station.

title: During an external call Press and hold to initiate an internal call. $\\hookrigh
text: # During an external call Press and hold to initiate an internal call. $\\hookrightarrow$ The external caller is put on hold. $\\hookrightarrow$ For multi-handset versions, the handsets available for intercom are displayed, then go to step 2. $\\hookrightarrow$ For two-handset versions, the other handset rings, then go to step 3. Select a handset, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The selected handset rings. Press on the selected handset. $\\hookrightarrow$ The intercom is established.

title: Press [Conf] . $\\hookrightarrow$ You are now in a 3-way conference call with an
text:
# Press [Conf] . $\\hookrightarrow$ You are now in a 3-way conference call with an $\\ominus\ imes$ ternal call and a selected handset. Press to end the conference call and transfer the existing call to the selected handset.
\u2022\u2022 Press to join an ongoing conference with another handset if [Services] $>$ [Conference] is set to [Auto] .

title: During the conference call 1 Press [Int.] to put the external call on hold and g
text: # During the conference call 1 Press [Int.] to put the external call on hold and go back to the internal call. $\\hookrightarrow$ The external call is put on hold. 2 Press [Conf] to establish the conference call again. Note \u2022\u2022 If a handset hangs up during the conference call, the other handset remains connected to the external call.

title: 6 Text and numbers You can enter text and numbers for handset name, phonebook re
text: # 6 Text and numbers You can enter text and numbers for handset name, phonebook records, and other menu items.

title: Enter text and numbers 1 Press once or several times on the alphanumeric key to
text: # Enter text and numbers 1 Press once or several times on the alphanumeric key to enter the selected character. 2 Press [Clear] to delete a character. Press and hold [Clear] to delete all characters. Press and to move the cursor left and right. 3 Press to add a space. \u2022\u2022 For information on key mapping of characters and numbers, see the chapter 'Appendix'.

title: Switch between uppercase and lowercase By default, the first letter of each word
text: # Switch between uppercase and lowercase By default, the first letter of each word in a sentence is uppercase and the rest is lowercase. Press and hold $\\mathbf{\\Pi}_{a/\\mathsf{A}}^{\\#}$ to switch between the uppercase and lowercase letters.

title: 7 Phonebook This phone has a phonebook that stores up to 50 records. Each record
text: # 7 Phonebook This phone has a phonebook that stores up to 50 records. Each record can have a name up to 16 characters long. Each record can store up to 2 numbers, with each number up to 24 digits long.

title: Direct keys The phone also features 3 direct keys to store the emergency phone n
text: # Direct keys The phone also features 3 direct keys to store the emergency phone numbers or numbers you call frequently.

title: Set the direct keys 1 Enter the number. 2 Press and hold m1 , m2 or m3 . 3 Enter
text: # Set the direct keys 1 Enter the number. 2 Press and hold m1 , m2 or m3 . 3 Enter/edit the name, then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Enter/edit the number, then press [Save] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The direct key is set.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 If there is already a record saved in the direct key, you need
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 If there is already a record saved in the direct key, you need to confirm whether you want to replace the old record with the new one. \u2022\u2022 The phonebook records and the direct key records are saved in the base station. For multiple handset versions, the same phonebook records and direct key records are shared among different handsets.

title: Direct access memories You have up to 2 direct access memories (Keys 1 and 2). D
text: # Direct access memories You have up to 2 direct access memories (Keys 1 and 2). Depending on your country, keys 1 and 2 are preset to the voice mail number and information service number of your service provider respectively. When you press and hold on the key in standby mode, the saved phone number is dialed automatically.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 The availability of direct access memory is country dependent.
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 The availability of direct access memory is country dependent.

title: View the phonebook
text: # View the phonebook

title: Note \u2022\u2022 You can view the phonebook on one handset only each time. 1 Pr
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 You can view the phonebook on one handset only each time. 1 Press or select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [View] $>$ [OK] to access the phonebook list. 2 Select a contact and view the available information.

title: Search a record You can search the phonebook records in these ways: \u2022 Scrol
text: # Search a record You can search the phonebook records in these ways: \u2022 Scroll the contacts list. \u2022 Enter the first character of the contact name.

title: Scroll the contact list 1 Press or select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>\\mathsf{[V i
text: # Scroll the contact list 1 Press or select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>\\mathsf{[V i e w]}>\\mathsf{[O K]}$ to access the phonebook list. 2 Press and $\\blacktriangledown$ to scroll through the phonebook list.

title: Enter the first character of a contact 1 Press or select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook]
text:
# Enter the first character of a contact 1 Press or select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [View] $>$ [OK] to access the phonebook list.
2 Press the alpha numerical key that matches the character. $\\hookrightarrow$ The first record that starts with this character is displayed.

title: Call from the phonebook Press or select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [View] $>$ [O
text: # Call from the phonebook Press or select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [View] $>$ [OK] to access the phonebook list. Select a contact in the phonebook list. Press [View] . Select a type of number (mobile/home/ office), press $\\backsim$ to make the call.

title: Access the phonebook during a call 1 Press [Option] and select [Phonebook] . 2 P
text: # Access the phonebook during a call 1 Press [Option] and select [Phonebook] . 2 Press [OK] to confirm. 3 Select a contact, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The number is displayed.

title: Add a record \u2022\u2022 If your phonebook memory is full, a notification messa
text: # Add a record \u2022\u2022 If your phonebook memory is full, a notification message is displayed on the handset. Delete some records to add new ones. \u2022\u2022 When you change the number of a record, the new number will overwrite the old number. Select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [Add new] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Enter the name, then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Enter the mobile number, home number and office number (choose either two of them), then press [Save] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ Your new record is saved.

Press / to move the cursor left and right.

title: Edit a record 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [Edit] , then press [OK] to co
text: # Edit a record 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [Edit] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select a contact, then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Edit the name, then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Select the mobile/home/office number, then press [OK] to confirm. 5 Edit the number, then press [Save] to confirm.

title: Delete a record 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [Delete] , then press [OK] t
text: # Delete a record 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [Delete] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select a contact, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ A confirmation request is displayed on the handset. 3 Press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The record is deleted.

title: Delete all records 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [Delete all] , press [OK]
text: # Delete all records 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phonebook] $>$ [Delete all] , press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ A confirmation request is displayed on the handset. 2 Press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ All records ( $\\left(\\mathrm{\\mathbf{e}}\ imes\\mathrm{\\mathbf{\\rho}}\\right)$ cept the 2 direct access memory records) are deleted.

title: 8 Call log The call log stores the incoming call history of all missed, received
text: # 8 Call log The call log stores the incoming call history of all missed, received or blocked calls. The incoming call history includes the name and number of the caller, call time and date. This feature is available if you have registered to the caller ID service with your service provider. Your phone can store up to 50 incoming call records. The call log icon on the handset flashes to remind you of any unanswered calls. If the caller allows the display of his identity, you can view his name or number. The call records are displayed in chronological order with the most recent received call at the top of the list.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 Make sure that the number in the call list is valid before you
text:
# Note \u2022\u2022 Make sure that the number in the call list is valid before you can call back directly from the call list. The icons shown on the screen tell you whether they are missed/received/blocked calls.
[[PIC_MISSING]]

text: # Call list type You can set if you can see all incoming calls or only missed calls from the call log.

text: # View the call records Press . $\\hookrightarrow$ The incoming call log is displayed. Select a record, then press [Option] . Select [V $\\mathsf{i e w]}>\\mathsf{[O K]}$ for more available information.

Enter and edit the name, then press [OK] to confirm. Select a type of number (mobile/home/ office), then press [OK] to confirm. Edit the number, then press [Save] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The record is saved.

text: # Delete a call record 1 Press . $\\hookrightarrow$ The incoming call log is displayed. 2 Select a record, then press [Option] . 3 Select [Delete] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ A confirmation request is displayed on the handset. 4 Press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The record is deleted.

title: Delete all call records 1 Press . $\\hookrightarrow$ The incoming call log is di
text: # Delete all call records 1 Press . $\\hookrightarrow$ The incoming call log is displayed. 2 Press [Option] to enter the options menu. 3 Select [Delete all] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ A confirmation request is displayed on the handset. 4 Press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ All records are deleted.

title: Access the redial list during a call The redial list stores the call history of
text: # Access the redial list during a call The redial list stores the call history of dialed calls. It includes the names and/or numbers you have called. This phone can store up to 20 redial records.

title: View the redial records Select [Redial] .
text: # View the redial records Select [Redial] .

title: Save a redial record to the phonebook 1 Press [Redial] to enter the list of dial
text: # Save a redial record to the phonebook 1 Press [Redial] to enter the list of dialed calls. 2 Select a record, press [OK] , then press [Option] . 3 Select [Save number] , then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Enter and edit the name, then press [OK] to confirm. 5 Select a type of number (mobile/home/ office), then press [OK] to confirm. 6 Edit the number, then press [Save] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The record is saved. 1 Press [Option] and select [Redial] . 2 Press [OK] to confirm.

title: Delete a redial record Press [Redial] to enter the list of dialed calls. Select
text: # Delete a redial record Press [Redial] to enter the list of dialed calls. Select a record, then press [Option] . Select [Delete] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ A confirmation request is displayed on the handset. Press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The record is deleted.

title: Delete all redial records Press [Redial] to enter the list of dialed calls. Sele
text: # Delete all redial records Press [Redial] to enter the list of dialed calls. Select [Option] $>$ [Delete all] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ A confirmation request is displayed on the handset. Press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ All records are deleted.

title: Redial a call 1 Press [Redial] . 2 Select the record you want to call. Press . $
text: # Redial a call 1 Press [Redial] . 2 Select the record you want to call. Press . $\\hookrightarrow$ The number is dialed out.

title: 10 Phone settings You can customize the settings to make it your own phone.
text: # 10 Phone settings You can customize the settings to make it your own phone.

title: Sound settings
text: # Sound settings

title: Audio boost
text: # Audio boost

title: Caution \u2022\u2022 Audio boost should only be used by people with hearing impa
text: # Caution \u2022\u2022 Audio boost should only be used by people with hearing impairment. \u2022\u2022 For hearing safety, audio boost will be deactivated at the end of each call. This feature can amplify the sound volume in earpiece or handsfree mode. Press to activate/deactivate audio boost during a call. When audio boost is activated, the LED indicator on the handset will stay steadily on. [Audio boost on] will be displayed.

title: Set the hearing aid compatibility Your phone is hearing aid compatible (accordin
text: # Set the hearing aid compatibility Your phone is hearing aid compatible (according to the standard ETS300381). This enables the phone to couple with the hearing aid device to amplify the sound and reduce noise interference. Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [MySound] $>$ [Earpiece] $>$ [HAC] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Set the sound profile You can set the sound in the earpiece or loudspeaker among
text: # Set the sound profile You can set the sound in the earpiece or loudspeaker among 3 different profiles. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [MySound] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [Earpiece] / [Loudspeaker] . 3 Select a profile, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. Access the sound profile during a call Press [Sound] for once or several times to change the sound profile during a call.

title: Set the handset's ringtone volume You can select among 5 ringtone volume levels
text: # Set the handset's ringtone volume You can select among 5 ringtone volume levels or [Off] . 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [Ring volume] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select a volume level, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Set the handset's ringtone You can select from 10 ringtones. 1 Select [Menu] $>$
text: # Set the handset's ringtone You can select from 10 ringtones. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [Ring tones] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select a ringtone, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Set the visual ring When the visual ring is activated, the display backlight wil
text: # Set the visual ring When the visual ring is activated, the display backlight will flash when there is an incoming call. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [Visual ring] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [Flashing on] / [Flashing off] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Silent mode You can set your phone to silent mode and enable the silent mode for
text: # Silent mode You can set your phone to silent mode and enable the silent mode for a specified duration. When the silent mode is turned on, your phone does not ring and does not sound the key tone and docking tone.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 When you press to find your handset, or when you activate the
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 When you press to find your handset, or when you activate the alarm, your phone still sends alert even when the silent mode is activated. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [Silent mode] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select $\\mathsf{[O n/o f f]}>\\mathsf{[O n]/[O f f]}$ , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. 3 Select [Start & end] , then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Set the time, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. $\\hookrightarrow$ \u00bb is displayed.

title: Set the key tone Key tone is the sound made when you press a key on the handset.
text: # Set the key tone Key tone is the sound made when you press a key on the handset. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [Key tone] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select $[\\mathsf{O n}]/[\\mathsf{O f f}]$ , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [Docking tone] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [On] / [Off] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Set the battery tone Battery tone is the sound made when the battery is low and
text: # Set the battery tone Battery tone is the sound made when the battery is low and needs recharging. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Sounds] $>$ [Battery tone] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [On] / [Off] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Eco mode The ECO mode reduces the transmission power of the handset and base sta
text: # Eco mode The ECO mode reduces the transmission power of the handset and base station when you are on a call or when the phone is in standby mode. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Eco mode] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [On] / [Off] , and press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. $\\hookrightarrow$ ECO is displayed in standby mode.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 When ECO mode is set to [On] , the connection range between th
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 When ECO mode is set to [On] , the connection range between the handset and the base station can be reduced.

title: Set the docking tone Docking tone is the sound made when you place the handset o
text: # Set the docking tone Docking tone is the sound made when you place the handset on the base station or charger.

title: ECO $^+$ mode When the $\\mathsf{E C O+}$ mode is activated, it eliminates the r
text: # ECO $^+$ mode When the $\\mathsf{E C O+}$ mode is activated, it eliminates the radiation of the handset and base station in standby mode. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [ECO+ mode] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [On] / [Off] , and press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. $\\hookrightarrow\\mathsf{E C O}+$ is displayed in standby mode after a while.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 Make sure that all the handsets registered to the base station
text:
# Note \u2022\u2022 Make sure that all the handsets registered to the base station are $\ imes\\mathsf{L490}/\\mathsf{X}\\mathsf{L495}$ in order to have the $\\mathsf{E C O+}$ feature functioning properly. \u2022\u2022 When $\\mathsf{E C O}+\\mathsf{\\Delta}$ is activated, the standby time is reduced. This is because in $\\mathsf{E C O}+\\mathsf{\\Delta}$ mode the base station is not transmitting any signal in standby mode;
therefore the handset needs to \u201clisten\u201d more frequently for signals from the base station to detect incoming calls or other requests from the base station. The time it takes for the handset to access features like call setup, call log, paging, and phonebook browsing is also delayed. The handset will not alert you to link loss in case of power loss or moving out of range. The following table shows you the current status of the handset screen with different ECO mode and $\\mathsf{E C O+}$ mode settings.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
[[PIC_MISSING]]

title: Name the handset The name of the handset can be up to 14 characters. It is displ
text: # Name the handset The name of the handset can be up to 14 characters. It is displayed on the handset screen in standby mode. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Phone name] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Enter or edit the name. To erase a character, select [Clear] . 3 Press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Set the date and time For information, see 'Set the date and time (see 'Set the
text: # Set the date and time For information, see 'Set the date and time (see 'Set the date and time' on page 9)'. Set the display language

title: Note \u2022\u2022 This feature only applies to models with multiplelanguage supp
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 This feature only applies to models with multiplelanguage support. \u2022\u2022 Languages available vary from country to country. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Language] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select a language, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Auto answer When you activate the auto answer feature, you can connect to the in
text: # Auto answer When you activate the auto answer feature, you can connect to the incoming call automatically once you pick up the handset. When you deactivate this feature, you have to press $\\backsim$ or to answer the incoming call. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [Auto answer] , then select [OK] to confirm.

title: Set the handset LED indicator behavior You can set the LED indicator behavior on
text:
# Set the handset LED indicator behavior You can set the LED indicator behavior on your handset for different events or charging status. Select [Menu] $>$ [Phone setup] $>$ [LED status] $>$ [Events status] / [Charge status ] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. The table below shows you the current status with different LED indicator behavior on the handset.

title: 11 Alarm clock Your phone has a built-in alarm clock. Check the information belo
text: # 11 Alarm clock Your phone has a built-in alarm clock. Check the information below to set your alarm clock.

title: Set the alarm 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Alarm] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select
text:
# Set the alarm 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Alarm] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [On once] or [On daily] , then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Enter an alarm time, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The alarm is set and is displayed on the screen.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 Press / to switch between [am] / [pm] (Country dependent). Turn off the alarm When the alarm rings Press any key to turn off the alarm. Before the alarm rings Select [Menu] $>\\mathsf{[A l a r m]}>\\mathsf{[O f f]}$ , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: 12 Telephone answering machine
text: # 12 Telephone answering machine

title: Note
text:
# Note
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 Available only for XL495. Your phone includes a telephone answering machine that records unanswered calls when it is on. You can access the answering machine remotely and change the settings through the answering machine menu on the handset. The button on the base station lights up when the answering machine is on.

title: Turn the answering machine on or off You can turn the answering machine on or of
text: # Turn the answering machine on or off You can turn the answering machine on or off through the base station or the handset.

title: Through the handset 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>[\\mathsf{O n}/$ off]
text:
# Through the handset 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>[\\mathsf{O n}/$ off] $>[\\mathsf{O n}]/[\\mathsf{O f f}]$ , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 When the answering machine is on, select [Answer only] / [Record also] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.
[[PIC_MISSING]]
\u2022\u2022 [Answer only] means calls are only answered, messages are not recorded. \u2022\u2022 [Record also] means calls are answered and messages are recorded.

title: Through the base Press $\\odot$ to turn the answering machine on or off in stand
text: # Through the base Press $\\odot$ to turn the answering machine on or off in standby mode.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 When the answering machine is switched on, it answers incoming
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 When the answering machine is switched on, it answers incoming calls after a certain number of rings based on the ring delay setting.

title: Family notes You can leave your family an audio note in the answering machine. 1
text: # Family notes You can leave your family an audio note in the answering machine. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Family note] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Start recording close to the microphone after the beep. 3 Press [OK] to stop recording. 4 Press [OK] to listen to the audio note. 5 Press [OK] to save the family note or [Delete] to remove it.

title: Set the answering machine language
text: # Set the answering machine language

title: Note \u2022\u2022 This feature only applies to models with multiplelanguage supp
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 This feature only applies to models with multiplelanguage support. The answering machine language is the language for the announcements. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Voice language] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select a language, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Set the answer mode You can set the answering machine and select if the callers
text: # Set the answer mode You can set the answering machine and select if the callers can leave messages. Select [Record also] if you allow the callers to leave messages. Select [Answer only] if you forbid the callers to leave messages. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Voice answer] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select an answer mode, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. \u2022\u2022 If the memory is full, the answering machine switches to [Answer only] mode automatically.

title: Announcements The announcement is the message your caller hears when the answeri
text: # Announcements The announcement is the message your caller hears when the answering machine picks up the call. The answering machine has 2 pre-recorded announcements: [Record also] and [Answer only] .

title: Record an announcement The maximum length of the announcement you can record is
text: # Record an announcement The maximum length of the announcement you can record is 3 minutes. The newly recorded announcement automatically replaces the old one. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Announcement] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [Answer only] or [Record also], then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Select [Record] , then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Start recording close to the microphone after the beep. 5 Press [OK] to stop recording or the recording stops after 3 minutes. $\\hookrightarrow$ You can listen to the newly recorded announcement on the handset.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 Make sure that you speak close to the microphone when you reco
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 Make sure that you speak close to the microphone when you record an announcement.

title: Listen to the announcement 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Announcement
text: # Listen to the announcement 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Announcement] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [Answer only] or [Record also], then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Select [Play] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ You can listen to the current announcement.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 You can no longer listen to the announcement when you pick up
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 You can no longer listen to the announcement when you pick up an incoming call.

title: Restore the default announcement 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Announ
text: # Restore the default announcement 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Announcement] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [Record also] or [Answer only] , then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Select [Use default] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The default announcement is restored.

title: Incoming messages Each message can be up to 3 minutes long. When you receive new
text:
# Incoming messages Each message can be up to 3 minutes long. When you receive new messages, the new
message indicator flashes on the answering machine and the handset.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 If you pick up the phone when the caller leaves a message, the
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 If you pick up the phone when the caller leaves a message, the recording stops and you can talk to the caller directly. \u2022\u2022 If the memory is full, the answering machine switches to [Answer only] automatically. Delete old messages to receive new ones.

title: Listen to the incoming messages You can listen to the incoming messages in the s
text: # Listen to the incoming messages You can listen to the incoming messages in the sequence they are recorded.

title: From the base \u2022 To start/stop listening, press . \u2022 To adjust the volum
text: # From the base \u2022 To start/stop listening, press . \u2022 To adjust the volume, press / . \u2022 To play the previous message/replay the current message, press . \u2022 To play the next message, press . \u2022 To delete the current message, press . \u2022\u2022 No deleted messages can be recovered.

title: From the handset You can listen to the incoming messages in the sequence they ar
text: # From the handset You can listen to the incoming messages in the sequence they are recorded. \u2022 If there are new messages, press to listen. If there is no new message, press , then [OK] to listen to old messages. \u2022 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Play] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ New messages start playing. Old messages start playing if there is no new message. \u2022 Press [Back] to stop listening. \u2022 Press [OK] to enter the options menu. \u2022 Press / to increase/decrease the volume.

title: Delete an incoming message From the base Press when you listen to the message. $
text: # Delete an incoming message From the base Press when you listen to the message. $\\hookrightarrow$ The current message is deleted.

title: From the handset 1 When you listen to the message, press [Option] to enter the o
text: # From the handset 1 When you listen to the message, press [Option] to enter the options menu. 2 Select [Delete] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The current message is deleted.

title: Delete all old incoming messages Press and hold on the base station in standby m
text: # Delete all old incoming messages Press and hold on the base station in standby mode. $\\hookrightarrow$ All old messages are permanently deleted.

title: From the handset 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Delete all] , then pre
text: # From the handset 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Delete all] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ A confirmation request is displayed on the handset. 2 Press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ All old messages are permanently deleted.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 You can only delete the read messages. No deleted messages can
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 You can only delete the read messages. No deleted messages can be recovered.

title: Call screening You can hear the caller while he is leaving a message. Press $\\b
text: # Call screening You can hear the caller while he is leaving a message. Press $\\backsim$ to answer the call. From the base Press / to adjust the speaker volume during call screening.

title: Note
text: # Note

text: # \u2022\u2022 If you turn the speaker to the lowest volume level, the call screening function is off.

title: Set the ring delay You can set the number of times the phone rings before the an
text: # Set the ring delay You can set the number of times the phone rings before the answering machine picks up the call. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Answ. Machine] $>$ [Ring delay] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select a new setting, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved. \u2022\u2022 Make sure that the answering machine is switched on before this feature can be applied.

title: Tip \u2022\u2022 When you access the answering machine remotely, you are recomme
text:
# Tip \u2022\u2022 When you access the answering machine remotely, you are recommended to set the ring delay to [Toll saver] mode. This is a cost effective way to manage your messages. When there are new messages, the answering machine picks up the call after 3 rings;
When there is no messages, it picks up the call after 5 rings.

title: Remote access You can operate your answering machine when you are away from home
text: # Remote access You can operate your answering machine when you are away from home. Simply make a call to your phone with a touch tone phone and enter your 4-digit PIN code.

\u2022\u2022 The remote access PIN code is the same as your system PIN. The preset PIN is 0000.

text: # Activate/Deactivate auto conference 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Conference] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select [Auto] / [Off] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Call barring You can block outgoing calls to numbers, such as overseas calls or
text: # Call barring You can block outgoing calls to numbers, such as overseas calls or undesirable hotlines by barring calls that start with certain digits. You can create 4 sets of numbers with 4 digits at maximum for each set of numbers. When you make a call that starts with one of these 4 sets of numbers, the outgoing call is then barred.

text: # Select the barring mode 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Call barring] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Enter the call barring PIN/passcode. Then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Select [Barring mode] , then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Select [On] / [Off] , then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

text: # Add new numbers to the barred list 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Call barring] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Enter the call barring $\\mathsf{P}\\Vert\\mathsf{N},$ /passcode. The default PIN is 0000. Then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Select [Barring number] , then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Select a number from the list, then press [OK] to confirm. 5 Edit the number, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

The default PIN is 0000. Then press [OK] to confirm. 5 Enter the new PIN/passcode, then press [OK] to confirm. 6 Enter the new PIN/passcode again, then press [Save] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Call blocking There are two ways to block incoming calls: \u2022 block anonymous

text: # Note \u2022\u2022 Make sure you have subscribed to the caller ID service before you use this feature.

title: Block anonymous calls You can block calls without identity in order to reduce un
text: # Block anonymous calls You can block calls without identity in order to reduce unwanted calls, like calls from tele marketers. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Call block] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Enter the call blocking PIN/passcode. The default PIN is 0000. Then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Select [Anonymous call] $>[\\mathsf{O n}]/[\\mathsf{O f f}]$ to activate/deactivate call blocking, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Blacklist You can put numbers into the blacklist to block incoming calls from ce
text: # Blacklist You can put numbers into the blacklist to block incoming calls from certain undesirable numbers. You can create 4 sets of numbers with 24 digits at maximum for each set of numbers. When there is an incoming call that starts with one of these 4 sets of numbers, the ringer will be muted.

title: Note \u2022\u2022 The contact's name in phonebook will not be displayed if this
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 The contact's name in phonebook will not be displayed if this contact's number matches the record saved in the blacklist.

text: # Create a blacklist 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Call block] then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Enter the call blocking $\\mathsf{P}\\Vert\\mathsf{N},$ /passcode. The default PIN is 0000. title: Change the call blocking PIN/ passcode 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Call
text:
# Change the call blocking PIN/ passcode 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Call block] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Enter the call blocking $\\mathsf{P}\\Vert\\mathsf{N}\\Vert/$ /passcode. The default $\\mathsf{P}\\|\\mathsf{N}$ is 0000. Then press [OK] to confirm.
3 Select [Change PIN], then press [OK] to confirm. 4 Enter the old PIN/passcode. The default PIN is 0000. Then press [OK] to confirm. 5 Enter the new PIN/passcode, then press [OK] to confirm. 6 Enter the new PIN/passcode again, then press [Save] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Auto prefix This feature checks and formats your outgoing call number before it
text:
# Auto prefix This feature checks and formats your outgoing call number before it is dialed out. The prefix number can replace the detect number you set in the menu. For example, you set 604 as the detect number and 1250 as the prefix. When you have dialed out a number such as 6043338888, your phone changes the number to 12503338888 when it dials out.
[[PIC_MISSING]]

title: Note \u2022\u2022 The maximum length of a detect number is 10 digits. The maximu
text: # Note \u2022\u2022 The maximum length of a detect number is 10 digits. The maximum length of an auto prefix number is 10 digits. \u2022\u2022 This is a country dependent feature.

title: Set auto prefix 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Auto prefix] , then press [O
text: # Set auto prefix 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Auto prefix] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Enter the detect number, then press [OK] to confirm. 3 Enter the prefix number, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Select the recall duration Make sure that the recall time is set correctly befor
text: # Select the recall duration Make sure that the recall time is set correctly before you can answer a second call. In normal case, the phone is already preset for the recall duration. You can select among 3 options: [Short] , [Medium] and [Long] . The number of available options varies with different countries. For details, consult your service provider. 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Recall time] , then press [OK] to confirm. 2 Select an option, then press [OK] to confirm. $\\hookrightarrow$ The setting is saved.

title: Dial mode
text:
# Dial mode
[[PIC_MISSING]]
Note \u2022\u2022 This feature only applies to models that support both tone and pulse dial. Dial mode is the telephone signal used in your country. The phone supports tone (DTMF) and pulse (rotary) dial. Consult the service provider for detailed information.

title: Set the dial mode 1 Select [Menu] $>$ [Services] $>$ [Dial mode]then press [OK]
text:
